title: acer tataricum subsp. ginnala
intro: Acer tataricum subsp. ginnala, also known as Amur maple or Ginnala, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, belonging to the family Sapindaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Acer tataricum subsp. ginnala include reducing fever, relieving respiratory problems, and aiding in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark, leaves, and roots are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, including colds and flu.
